active_event.title: U.S.-Iran war and regional escalation
active_event.note: This event lens is manual by design. The monitor treats the war as the cause, the transmission channels as the confounded middle, and the macro conclusions as the object that is updated daily.

scenarios.title: Inflation shock
scenarios.description: War escalation persists mainly through energy, shipping, and input costs, keeping inflation compensation firm and delaying policy relief even while growth damage stays limited.

scenarios.title: Stagflation
scenarios.description: The war shock broadens from energy and shipping into inflation persistence, tighter financial conditions, and weaker growth sentiment at the same time.

scenarios.title: Growth slowdown / recession risk
scenarios.description: The war shock evolves mainly into weaker demand, tighter risk conditions, and lower yields, with growth damage overtaking the initial commodity inflation impulse.

scenarios.title: Contained shock
scenarios.description: War-driven risk premium remains visible but policy response, market adaptation, and fading tightness stop the shock from becoming a durable macro regime.
